member.name: Peter Swallow
member.synopsis: Peter Swallow is the Labour MP for Bracknell , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Rented Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether he will publish data on the disposal of social housing stock broken down by tenure and local authority area.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Large Private Registered Providers (PRPs) of social housing (i.e. those with 1,000 or more social and affordable homes) are required to report on the number of sales, demolitions, new builds, acquisitions or movements of owned units of social and affordab...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Families: Bracknell Forest
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps she is taking to support families in Bracknell Forest in summer 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to supporting families. At the Budget, we took £150 off energy bills, froze rail fares and prescription charges, and lifted 450,000 children out of poverty. With our Great British Summer Savings scheme, we are making bus trave...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ataxia
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the accessibility of TG6 blood tests for diagnosis of gluten ataxia.
writtenQuestions.answerText: While National Health Service specialised services commissions care for some rare ataxias, gluten ataxia is commissioned within integrated care board-commissioned neurology services. Decisions on the provision of diagnostic tests, including blood tests, a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband and Mobile Phones: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether her Department is taking steps to help tackle loyalty penalties for phone and broadband customers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As regulator for telecoms, Ofcom has introduced a range of measures to improve transparency and consumer engagement in telecoms markets, including clearer end-of-contract notifications, new rules on in-contract price rises, and the One Touch Switching pro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what recent progress he has made on consulting on reform to the calculation of Child maintenance.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We will announce further details in due course.Given the significant amount of time since the child maintenance calculation was updated, we need to assess carefully the impact of any proposed changes on all parents that use the Child Maintenance Service (...

writtenQuestions.heading: Driving Tests: Waiting Lists
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what progress has her Department made on helping to reduce the waiting time for driving test appointments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Driver and Vehicle Standards Agency’s (DVSA) main priority is upholding road safety standards while it works hard to reduce car practical driving test waiting times. The agency is intensifying its efforts to reduce waiting times and improve access to ...

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Pay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what progress he has made on providing the NHS staff council with a funded mandate to make improvements to the Agenda for Change pay structure.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The NHS Staff Council was provided with a mandate to negotiate changes to the Agenda for Change pay structure for their agreement on 5 March 2026.Pay structure changes that are agreed by the NHS Staff Council under the mandate will deliver additional pay ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Animals: Abuse
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department is considering measures to restrict those convicted of animal abuse from purchasing animals.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Post‑conviction sentencing powers under the Animal Welfare Act 2006 already include disqualification orders, which prevent individuals convicted of animal cruelty from owning, keeping, or participating in the care of animals. Where police or local authori...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Transport
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she has taken to consult with groups representing motorcyclists to inform the Integrated National Transport Strategy for England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department has conducted extensive research and engagement with stakeholders and members of the public to inform the strategy. We have heard directly from motorcyclists and motorcycle representative groups, including the Motorcycle Action Group, throu...

writtenQuestions.heading: Tax Avoidance: Prosecutions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps she is taking to implement the recommendation of the Independent Loan Charge Review 2025 on holding promoters of loan charge schemes to account.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Government’s response to the Loan Charge Review, since the Loan Charge was introduced, HMRC’s approach to tackling promoters has become far more robust. The Government is also introducing new powers in Finance Bill 2025/26 to close in on...

writtenQuestions.heading: Elections: Subversion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps he is taking with Cabinet colleagues to help protect democratic processes from foreign interference.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Protecting UK democratic processes from foreign interference is a top priority for the Government. The Joint Election Security and Preparedness Unit coordinates work across government to respond to a range of threats to elections, including foreign interf...

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of Ofgem's powers to regulate energy companies and protect customers from overpayment on energy bills.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is carrying out a review of Ofgem to re-establish it as a strong consumer champion, driving up standards for household and business consumers. The outcome of the review will be published shortly. The Government has also consulted on strengt...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Software
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to improve patient experience of the NHS App.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The NHS App is at the forefront of a major digital transformation and will revolutionise access to healthcare by putting patients at the centre of a modern, personalised, and data-driven service. One of the key aims is to empower individuals with greater ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Business: Billing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, if he will take steps to ensure that businesses pay for goods and services, including those from freelancers, on time.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government will legislate to end the scourge of late payments, which costs the UK economy £11bn per year and closes down 38 UK businesses every day. On 23 October we concluded a 12-week public consultation on our legislative proposals. We are now ana...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Dyslexia
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to support students with dyslexia in the context of the Curriculum and Assessment Review Final Report and the SEND white paper.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to ensuring that all pupils receive the support they need to achieve and thrive, including those with dyslexia. The Curriculum and Assessment Review emphasised inclusion and high standards for all, recommending evidence-led res...

writtenQuestions.heading: Clinical Trials: Bracknell Forest
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure that NHS patients in Bracknell Forest can access clinical trials.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There is a revolution taking place in medical science, and we want the next generation of treatments to be discovered, developed and distributed here in Britain. As set out in our 10-Year Health Plan, we will fast-track clinical trials set up times to 150...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Israel: Hamas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what diplomatic steps he is taking to help restore the ceasefire in Gaza.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We welcome the news that US-Israeli citizen Edan Alexander will be released. We thank Qatar and Egypt for their support in bringing Edan out. We call on all parties to use this opportunity to re-engage with negotiations to ensure a lasting ceasefire is im...

writtenQuestions.heading: Women: Football
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Wales, what steps she is taking with the Welsh Government to build on the participation of Wales at UEFA Women's Euro 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I’m thrilled that our Wales Women’s football team has qualified for the Euros for the first time, and I wish them all the very best for their games, especially the one against the Lionesses on 13 July. We are working closely with the Welsh Government to t...

writtenQuestions.heading: Chronic Illnesses: Travel Insurance

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is determined that insurers treat customers fairly and they are required to do so under Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) rules. Following its post-implementation review of its travel insurance signposting rules, the FCA set out a number of...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pancreatin
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent assessment he has made of the adequacy of the availability of Creon to treat Type 2 diabetes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has been working with suppliers to address current supply issues with pancreatic enzyme replacement therapy (PERT), including Creon, used by patients with conditions such as type 2 diabetes, cystic fibrosis, and certain cancers, including p...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
